reasoning: The series TOTLL (Loans and Leases in Bank Credit, All Commercial Banks) is highly trend-stationary with a consistent weekly upward trajectory. Over the last 15 weeks of data extracted, the series increased in 14 of the 15 weeks (93.3% hit rate). The average weekly increase over this period is +23 billion with a standard deviation of approximately 16.1 billion. Assuming normally distributed weekly increments, the Z-score for a non-increase is roughly -1.43, giving a ~7.6% chance of a decrease. This strongly aligns with the empirical 93% rate. Even accounting for possible end-of-quarter effects, minor unexpected pullbacks (such as the small -2 billion drop on Jan 28), and usual reporting revisions, a high probability of an increase is warranted. Therefore, I estimate a probability of 88% that the subsequent weekly figure will reflect an increase.
